meta_title: For Students: portfolio building to Solve stuck with basic apps (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to portfolio building and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: For Students: Portfolio Building to Solve Stuck with Basic Apps
content.seo_description: Break through the tutorial trap. Use this intermediate checklist to build high-impact AI portfolio projects using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Stop building 'to-do' lists. To move from intermediate to hireable, you must architect real-world solutions that solve actual business friction. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative refinement, you can transform abstract concepts into production-grade portfolio assets that command attention from senior technical recruiters.
content.intro:
You’ve finished the tutorials. You know the syntax, you’ve deployed a few static pages, but when you stare at a blank IDE, you feel the crushing weight of the 'tutorial trap.' Your current projects look like everyone else's, failing to demonstrate the engineering depth required to land that first high-impact role. You aren't lacking skill; you are lacking a system.

The transition from student to software engineer happens the moment you stop following guides and start building systems. This checklist is your blueprint to escape the basic-app cycle by integrating professional-grade tooling into your workflow, turning your GitHub into a repository of engineering competence.

steps.title: Define a Domain-Specific Problem
steps.description: Don't build a 'generic' app. Identify a manual process in a specific niche (e.g., automated legal doc summary or inventory tracking) to show you understand business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior System Architect. Help me define the MVP scope for a [Insert Niche] application. List 3 high-value features that solve a real manual bottleneck, emphasizing scalability and user-centric design.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Analyze the proposed architecture for [Project Name] and identify potential technical debt risks before I write the first line of code.'

steps.title: Architect the Data Flow
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate a robust schema. A strong portfolio project proves you understand how data moves, not just how it looks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a relational database schema for [Project Name] using PostgreSQL. Include indexing strategies for optimized read performance and explain the primary-foreign key relationships.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate the DDL scripts based on the schema and create a migration file to initialize the database.'

steps.title: Implement Modular Backend Services
steps.description: Break your app into micro-services. This demonstrates you can build maintainable codebases that aren't just one giant monolithic script.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a modular FastAPI backend structure for [Project Name]. Include dependency injection and robust error handling patterns suitable for a production environment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the core CRUD endpoints and integrate Pydantic models for request validation.'

steps.title: Integrate AI Contextual Awareness
steps.description: Add an AI layer that provides real value. Don't just slap a chat window on it; make the AI process your app's data.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a service that uses LLM-based extraction to analyze the data stored in my [Project Name] database and provide actionable insights for the user.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Write a robust service layer that handles LLM API calls with retry logic and token usage optimization.'

steps.title: Refine and Document for Impact
steps.description: Documentation is the difference between a student project and a professional showcase. Use Claude Code to polish your README.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a professional technical README for my GitHub repository. Highlight the 'Why' behind my architectural choices and the specific challenges I overcame.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Review the current codebase and generate a comprehensive documentation suite including API usage examples and setup instructions.'

pro_tips: Always build with a 'Production-First' mindset; treat your local environment as if it were a high-traffic production server.
Use Claude Code to perform 'Rubber Duck' debugging on your own architecture before committing code to ensure you understand every dependency.
Focus on error handling and edge cases; recruiters look for code that doesn't break when a user does something unexpected.
Maintain a detailed 'Architecture Decision Record' (ADR) in your repo to show you think critically about trade-offs.
Prioritize performance metrics; include a section in your README explaining how you optimized your database queries or API latency.
content.next_steps: Once your project is live, perform a 'Code Review' session with a peer or senior developer, then draft a blog post explaining your technical hurdles and how you solved them.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to build projects that actually matter. Stop settling for the ordinary and start shipping the exceptional. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
